overview: Cast noncreature spells to generate a swarm of 1/1 flying Spirits, using them to block, attack, or sacrifice for card advantage. Win by overwhelming opponents with board presence and out-drawing them through spell synergy, particularly against spell-heavy combo and token/artifact archetypes.
win_conditions: Abigale + token swarm, or a big removal/wipe to clear the board and attack with flying Spirits.
Use card draw to find finishers when needed.

keep_criteria: Keep hands with 2-3 lands, Abigale or early token generators (Lecturing Scornmage/Scolding Administrator), and at least one cheap noncreature spell (cantrip, removal, or tutor).
Prioritize hands that can cast a noncreature spell on turn 1 or 2.
mulligan_criteria: Mulligan if you have no lands, only expensive spells, or no way to generate initial value.

early_game: Turn 1: Play a land, cast a cheap cantrip or removal to trigger token generation.
Turn 2: Develop board with another land + spell, or play a Scornmage/Administrator to stabilize.
Sequence lands to ensure you can cast your first noncreature spell on curve.
Use early tokens to block aggressively or trade into opponent's threats.
mid_game: Prioritize casting noncreature spells over attacking with tokens unless necessary.
Use Spirit sacrifice for card draw when you're low on resources or facing a threat.
Manage mana to ensure you can respond to threats while maintaining spell flow.
Avoid overcommitting to the board without card advantage.
Commit threats when you have card advantage or removal to handle responses.
Hold back if you're vulnerable to board wipes or if you need to preserve Abigale for late-game value.
Use flying Spirits to control the air and force unfavorable blocks.
late_game: Abigale + token swarm, or a big removal/wipe to clear the board and attack with flying Spirits.
Use card draw to find finishers when needed.
Go all-in when you have card advantage, opponent is low on removal, or you can safely attack with multiple flying Spirits without risking Abigale.
Commit when you can trade tokens for value or close the game.

sequencing_tips: Abigale's ability triggers on cast, not on resolution, so it works with instant-speed spells.
Synergizes well with cheap cantrips and removal.
Avoid casting too many spells in a single turn if you can't follow up, as it leaves you vulnerable to wipes.
Common mistake: forgetting to sacrifice Spirits for draw when you're low on resources.
Unique interaction: Sacrificing a Spirit for draw can be done in response to a removal spell to replace it.

matchups.advice: B/W lacks hard counters, so use cheap, efficient spells, split casts, or sacrifice tokens to draw into answers. Use Thoughtseize/Duress to remove key counterspells. Cast spells in response to counters or use instant-speed cantrips to maintain pressure.
Apply early pressure, use removal on mana dorks, and don't overcommit to the board. Use board wipes to reset. Force them to interact with your board while they develop mana.
Stabilize with removal and board wipes. Use flying Spirits to block efficiently. Protect Abigale from early aggression. Trade tokens for their threats and slow the game down.

Pressure with spell synergy, use card draw to out-value them, and threaten with flying Spirits. Don't let them stabilize with cheap removal. Force them to use interaction on your spells while you build a board.

Value exchange is key. Use targeted removal on their Abigale or key engines. Prioritize card draw and board presence. Protect your commander. Win by out-resolving them in spell wars and trading efficiently.

common_threats: Watch for board wipes, targeted removal on Abigale, and opponent's mana sources.
Key tells: Opponent tapping for multiple colors often signals a big spell or wipe.
Track how many noncreature spells they've cast to anticipate their board state and plan your sacrifices accordingly.
